data.title: Amazon-Backed Nuclear Tech Firm X-Energy Raises $1 Billion in U.S. IPO
data.description:
By Kimberley Kao

Nuclear reactor developer X-Energy raised $1.02 billion in its U.S. initial public offering, coming as the artificial-intelligence boom drives demand for more power sources.

The Amazon-backed company said Thursday that it sold 44.3 million shares in an upsized offering at $23 each, above the guidance range of $16 to $19.

That implies a valuation of more than $9 billion, according to calculations based on prior exchange filings. Shares are expected to start trading on the Nasdaq on Friday.

The listing comes as the frenzied buildout of AI infrastructure fuels a massive need for more electricity to run energy-hungry data centers. As the technology advances, more and more companies are considering nuclear as a power source.

The first commercial nuclear-power projects in a decade are currently under construction in the U.S., including one by Bill Gates-backed TerraPower.

Facebook parent Meta recently said it plans to back nuclear projects with Oklo, TerraPower and Vistra, with other deals including Microsoft's power-purchase agreement with Constellation Energy to spur the restart of a nuclear plant in Pennsylvania, and a Google-NextEra Energy partnership to reopen a reactor in Iowa.

X-Energy said it is well-placed to capitalize on the rising demand from data centers, seeing scope for a big market for small modular reactors--compact "plug and play" versions of nuclear reactors that use fission to create electricity.

The company plans to use the proceeds from the IPO for working capital, future projects and general purposes like research and development.

In November last year, X-Energy closed a Series D financing round of about $700 million led by Jane Street, and including investors like Cathie Wood's ARK Invest.

In 2024, Amazon anchored a financing round of about $500 million for X-Energy, which said at the time that it was working with the tech giant to bring more than 5 gigawatts of new power projects online across the U.S. by 2039.

Founded in 2009, X-Energy develops advanced small modular nuclear reactors and fuel technology for clean energy generation.

Write to Kimberley Kao at kimberley.kao@wsj.com

data.title: Holcim Q1 Recurring EBIT Up 8.3% Organically; Confirms FY26 Guidance
data.description:
JONA (dpa-AFX) - Holcim (HLB.F, HOLN.SW) reported net sales of 3.52 billion Swiss francs in the first quarter, up 3.9% organically compared to the prior-year period, with momentum accelerating in March. Recurring EBIT grew 8.3% organically to 431 million francs, from the prior-year period.

Holcim said its NextGen Growth 2030 strategy is expected to continue to drive superior performance and value creation. Building on its strong results to start the year, Holcim confirmed its fiscal 2026 guidance.

At last close, Holcim shares were trading at 71.88 Swiss francs, up 0.62%.

data.title: U.S. Treasury Yields Edge Up as Oil Price Remain High — Market Talk
data.description: U.S. Treasury yields and the oil price edge higher in Asian trade with no meaningful progress between the U.S. and Iran toward a peace deal, even as neither seeks a re-escalation of the tensions. "U.S. Treasurys sold off a touch as oil drifted higher despite the ongoing cease-fire," Barclays Anshut Pradhan and Demi Hu say in a note. The two-year Treasury yield rises 1.7 basis points to 3.841%, while the 10-year yield is up 0.7 basis points to 4.329%, according to Tradeweb. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

data.title: Buy Sunteck Realty; target of Rs 520: Prabhudas Lilladher
data.description:
Prabhudas Lilladher's research report on Sunteck Realty

Sunteck Reality (SRIN) reported healthy pre-sales growth of 22% YoY, along with strong 39% YoY collection growth in Q4. SRIN’s proven ability to market ultra-luxury projects, aggressive and multi-pronged land acquisition capabilities in various micro markets across Mumbai Metropolitan Region (MMR) is an interesting play on Mumbai’s high value real estate market. We expect the company’s pre-sales to grow to 25% CAGR over FY26-28E, supported by launch acceleration, including the planned Dubai JV project in 2HFY27.

Outlook

Further given likely strong cash flow generation, we see SRIN to step up new project additions which will be a key catalyst for stock performance. Maintain ‘Buy’ rating with revised TP of Rs520share.

Disclaimer
